DIF_TIPO_1.sensor_signature: High raffination laser + elevated zone 5 combustion
DIF_TIPO_1.hypothesis: Surface defect caused by strip running high post-furnace under elevated zone 5 conditions
DIF_TIPO_1.confidence: Low — only 66 coils, weak cluster signal

DIF_TIPO_2.sensor_signature: High zone 1+2 furnace pressure, lower finishing force
DIF_TIPO_2.hypothesis: Pressure-related surface defect from over-pressurised early furnace zones
DIF_TIPO_2.confidence: Low — only 71 coils, weak cluster signal

DIF_TIPO_3.sensor_signature: High raffination laser + low pre-heat pressure + elevated zone 5
DIF_TIPO_3.hypothesis: Most prevalent defect — likely scale or oxidation driven by raffination strip position and insufficient pre-heating
DIF_TIPO_3.confidence: Medium — 429 coils, clearest cluster signal of all types

DIF_TIPO_4.sensor_signature: Almost always co-occurs with Tipo 3 — same sensor signature
DIF_TIPO_4.hypothesis: Secondary defect that accompanies Tipo 3 — possibly same root cause presenting differently on inspection
DIF_TIPO_4.confidence: Medium — 247 coils, but inseparable from Tipo 3 in sensor space

DIF_TIPO_5.hypothesis: Rare defect with no clear process driver in PLC data — may depend on material properties or external factors
DIF_TIPO_5.confidence: Very low — only 41 coils, no cluster signal

DIF_TIPO_6.sensor_signature: Weak association with high raffination laser + zone 5 pattern
DIF_TIPO_6.hypothesis: Rare defect loosely associated with same conditions as Tipo 3 and 4
DIF_TIPO_6.confidence: Very low — only 45 coils, very weak signal
